creator: Canada Research Council
description: A medical research project tracks antimicrobial resistance (AMR) through genomic surveillance across 215 NHS hospital sites. Project produces 156 publications, 31 genomic datasets (47,000+ bacterial isolates), 8 software tools. Registry must link publications to datasets (which papers used which data?) and show how surveillance enables real-time policy response.

Policy Impact: During COVID-19 pandemic, Canadian epidemiologists query this registry: "All bacterial genomic sequences from March 2020-2022" and discover secondary bacterial infection trends in hospitalized COVID patients. Findings directly inform hospital infection control policy. This real-time genomic intelligence is only possible because project-specific DC metadata links 156 papers + 31 datasets + surveillance protocols.
subject: Antimicrobial Resistance
Genomic Surveillance
Clinical Microbiology
Pathogen Genomics
title: AMR Genomic Surveillance (5 years, National)
